Tutorial - GMS2 Top Down Shooter

If you want to see tutorial in a separate web browser window click here.

Front End

Lets add the front end screen that comes up before the game starts. Create a new Sprite and press the Import button. Find the file in Included Files | TutorialResources | Sprites | spr_title_page.png in the browser (or click here). Call this sprite spr_title_page and leave the origin in the top left corner (do not alter it).

Create a new Room and call it rm_lvl_front_end. Change the Width and Height to 1000 by 1536. Drag this new room to the top of the list so it is the first room loaded.

Now we are going to use the sprite as a background. Select the Background Layer and click on no sprite. Load the newly imported background. You should now have a screen that looks like:

Run the game by pressing the Play Button. The room should now look like:

Create a new Sprite and import spr_press_space.png and call it spr_press_space. Find the file in Included Files | TutorialResources | Sprites in the browser (or click here).

Create a new game Object and bind the above sprite to it calling it obj_press_space.

Place the newly created object in rm_lvl_front_end on the Instances layer.

Run the game and now you should see the front end that still does nothing but has a call to action:

Lets make the press space call to action flash to attract the users attention. Create a new Step event script on obj_press_space and add:

obj_press_space: Step event

Run the game by pressing the Play Button. The call to action should be flashing:

Now lets add the ability to change rooms through the scripts which uses another GameMaker function room_goto(index). The argument index is the name of the room we assigned. In our case it is the rm_lvl_1 that we want to go to.

We will be checking for both a space bar and a start button. Ideally we would change this title text to have start instead of space, but maybe this is something you could do on your own!

Now add to the bottom of the obj_press_space: Step event.

obj_press_space: Step Event

Run the game by pressing the Play Button. Woops we get an error. It looks like it can't find the global variable.

This happens because it is looking for a variable in the room that is stored in obj_gamepad. We need to add the obj_gamepad to rm_lvl_front_end

rm_lvl_front_end

If you have a gamepad test the game. On my system the controller does not work in the game room. The reason is that this controller information gets stored at game bootup or any time the player removes or adds a controller. So the controller location stored in the front end screen is reset when entering the game level. How do we have obj_gamepad persist from level to level? There is a way.

First lets delete the obj_gamepad from rm_lvl_1. Select obj_gamepad on the layer you stored it on and delete it.

Double click and open obj_gamepad from the Resources menu. Select Persistent which will not delete this object from level to level so all the data stored in its variables do not get reset and persist.

Test the game and you should now be able to press space and you can go to the game screen.

Next up, we will implement lives.
